research_tasks.task_title: Replot the CsTi3Bi5 XRD pattern with indexed reflections
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel file, reproduce the diffraction plot associated with the crystal-structure characterization figure. Read the 2θ scan and intensity data, identify the main diffraction peaks, and generate a publication-style XRD plot with intensity versus 2θ. If the file contains any annotations or peak labels, preserve them; otherwise, add peak markers only if they can be determined directly from the data. The goal is to recreate the figure panel as faithfully as possible from the spreadsheet alone, including axis labels, units, and any visible indexing information present in the file.
execution_steps: Step_0: Load the spreadsheet and inspect the sheet structure, headers, and any embedded labels or metadata.
Step_1: Extract the 2θ and intensity columns, handling any merged cells or mixed text/numeric rows.
Step_2: Plot intensity versus 2θ using a clean line plot, matching the figure-style formatting as closely as possible.
Step_3: Detect prominent peaks and annotate them if the spreadsheet contains indexing information or if peak positions are unambiguous from the data.
Step_4: Export the replot as a figure panel suitable for comparison with the paper.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A re-created XRD diffraction pattern showing intensity as a function of 2θ, with the main reflections visible and any available indexing preserved.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot field-dependent magnetic susceptibility versus temperature
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided spreadsheet, reproduce the zero-field-cooled magnetic susceptibility curves measured under multiple applied magnetic fields. Extract the temperature axis and all magnetization/susceptibility traces, then generate a multi-curve plot with temperature on the x-axis and M on the y-axis. The plot should clearly distinguish the different field conditions listed in the file and preserve the overall figure layout used for a superconducting transition plot. If the spreadsheet includes paired columns or repeated headers, parse them carefully so each field trace is plotted correctly.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook structure and identify how the field-dependent traces are arranged across columns or rows.
Step_1: Parse the temperature values and the corresponding M values for each field condition.
Step_2: Plot all susceptibility traces on a single set of axes with a legend or labels for each magnetic field.
Step_3: Match the visual style of a superconducting transition figure, including axis labels, units, and consistent line styling.
Step_4: Save the resulting multi-trace plot for figure comparison.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A multi-curve susceptibility-versus-temperature plot showing the superconducting transition under several applied magnetic fields.

research_tasks.task_description: Using the spreadsheet, reproduce the low-field magnetic susceptibility plot containing multiple ultra-small applied fields. Extract the temperature axis and all M traces, then generate a multi-curve plot that shows how the superconducting shielding response evolves as the field is reduced. Preserve the field labels exactly as provided in the file and ensure the plot is formatted as a clean publication-style temperature sweep.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the spreadsheet to determine how the low-field traces are organized and labeled.
Step_1: Extract the temperature values and the corresponding magnetization/susceptibility values for each field.
Step_2: Plot all traces on the same axes, using a legend or direct labels for 0.01 Oe through 3 Oe.
Step_3: Format the axes and line styles to match a superconducting susceptibility figure.
Step_4: Export the final plot for figure reproduction.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A temperature-dependent susceptibility plot with multiple ultra-low-field traces, showing the field dependence of the superconducting response.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot resistivity versus temperature for two excitation currents
research_tasks.task_description: Using the transport spreadsheet, reproduce the in-plane resistance versus temperature figure. Extract the temperature axis and the resistance traces corresponding to the two current settings listed in the file, then plot R versus temperature with clear labels for each current. The resulting figure should show the metallic temperature dependence and the superconducting transition region, formatted as a standard transport measurement plot.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook and identify how the two current-dependent traces are encoded.
Step_1: Parse the temperature and resistance data for each current setting.
Step_2: Plot resistance versus temperature for both currents on the same axes or in the layout used by the source figure.
Step_3: Label the current conditions exactly as shown in the spreadsheet and format the axes with units.
Step_4: Save the replot for comparison with the paper figure.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A resistance-versus-temperature plot with two current-dependent traces and a visible superconducting transition.

research_tasks.task_title: Visualize the 2D grid data for the first spectroscopic map
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided spreadsheet containing a structured numeric grid, reconstruct the corresponding 2D map as an image-style plot. Determine the row and column ordering, convert the values into a matrix, and display the result using an appropriate color scale. If the file includes implicit axes or index columns, preserve them in the visualization. The objective is to reproduce the figure panel as a 2D intensity or spectroscopic map rather than a line plot.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the sheet to determine the grid dimensions, index columns, and any embedded axis labels.
Step_1: Convert the numeric table into a 2D array with the correct orientation.
Step_2: Render the array as an image/heatmap with a suitable colormap and colorbar.
Step_3: Add axis labels or tick labels if they can be inferred directly from the spreadsheet structure.
Step_4: Export the heatmap for figure comparison.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A 2D heatmap or image-style visualization reconstructed from the numeric grid data.
